problem:
The complete graph of $y=f(x)$, which consists of five line segments, is shown in red below. (On this graph, the distance between grid lines is $1$.)

What is the sum of the $x$-coordinates of all points where $f(x) = x+1$?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the graph of the function $y=f(x)$ based on the given information?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What are the equations of the five line segments that make up the graph of $y=f(x)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the $x$-coordinates of the points where $f(x) = x+1$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any restrictions on the values of $x$ for which $f(x) = x+1$? If so, what are they?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many points of intersection between the graph of $y=f(x)$ and the line $y=x+1$ are there?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we find the sum of the $x$-coordinates of all the points of intersection? If so, how?
classified_questions.label: b
